report:
## Your Health Assessment

Thank you for sharing your health picture with us. Your answers paint a clear story—and importantly, one that can be addressed as you prepare your body for this exciting next chapter.

### The Energy-Digestion Connection

Your energy level of 2/10 paired with the afternoon crash after lunch is telling us something important. When energy drops specifically *after* eating, it often signals that digestion itself is consuming more resources than it's providing—or that something in the digestive process is actively working against your energy production. Your bowel symptoms (excessive smell and messiness) support this: these are classic signs that food isn't being fully broken down and absorbed as it should be. Instead of fueling you, meals may be partially feeding gut bacteria that produce energy-draining byproducts.

### A Diet Working Against You

Your current eating pattern—while seemingly healthy on the surface—may actually be contributing to the problem. A raw salad for lunch is difficult to digest and provides little accessible energy, which could explain why your afternoons are particularly rough. Nuts, while nutritious in some ways, are high in fats that can suppress metabolic function over time. Your body may be running on stress hormones to compensate for inadequate fuel, then crashing when those hormones naturally dip after meals.

### What This Means for Your Pregnancy Goals

Here's the encouraging news: addressing energy metabolism and gut function *before* conception is one of the most impactful things you can do. Your body needs abundant, well-produced energy to support fertility and a healthy pregnancy. The patterns we're seeing are foundational—and foundational issues respond beautifully to the right approach.

---

These patterns often interconnect in ways that a comprehensive consultation can fully uncover. We'd love to explore your unique situation in more depth.

[Book a consultation](https://prism.miami/booking) to start your journey toward better health.
